All right, welcome back. Now it's time to script your YouTube ads. Now, you know, you're gonna be in like one of two camps here. You're gonna be like me and I have accepted the fact that I look like someone murdered Keith Richards and then revived his corpse and then that corpse really didn't take very good care of himself from a health perspective. And so I've just come to live with that and I'm not a perfectionist when it comes to being on camera or you might be like, oh my god, I have to look great. Everything's got to be perfect production wise. I'm here to tell you I'm gonna show you some of my ads during our time together here talking about YouTube. You do not have to look great. I mean seriously look at me and then when you see some of the ads Oh my god, like in some of the ads I was trying to use a fancy camera like I'm shooting literally everything on my just built-in What do you call it? Not laptop but my Mac book Pro thingy. It's not a pro. It's whatever the new stupid Macs are. It's not a Mac book. It's a Mac The color ones, you know, I have a blue one. Whatever the heck they're called. I'm blanking Mac Pro. I'm Mac. Good Lord That's the camera I'm using right so in some of the ads you'll see that actually worked I was trying to use a fancy Sony camera and have like that blurred background effect could not figure it out looked purple Just looked horrible ads still worked Okay, so that's thing number one just believes you do not have to look perfect Thing number two is you don't have to be on camera at all Words on a PowerPoint thing totally fine I've actually made ads where I just talked into the voice recorder app on my phone And then just had it transcribed and had the words put on the screen. So seriously like please Don't overthink the production value of the ads. Okay, what you say in the ads is important Production value really the more native and real it looks the better I mean, you don't want to like talk to somebody about like here's how to be successful in your you know if you're standing in front of like a Beat up trailer and you know, you got an old Camaro in the front yard No disrespect to people who live in trailers by the way I have lived in trailers in multiple Occasions on my life and actually didn't mind it. So, you know not hating but seriously, you know You want to look the part so to speak but what you're saying is really more important Ultimately, then you try to get the lighting perfect and all of that kind of stuff again You're gonna see some of my ads and you'll be like dear God man You need to see a doctor and then you might be like, yeah but this work because your frank her and everybody knows you and I would like to again bring your attention back to what I showed you Which is the targeting I show these ads of people who know me They're like in-market people for advertising services and stuff and I'm showing it to like the top 10% income in the United States Typically speaking that level of targeting, you know, so please don't overthink that.
